basics.name: Dengzhe Hou
basics.summary: Ph.D. Candidate in Information Sciences (Cognitive Neuroscience / Vision Science) at Tohoku University. JSPS DC2 Fellow. Research focuses on the brain mechanisms of self-initiated attentional control using EEG, eye-tracking, and computational modeling.

location.address: Graduate School of Information Sciences, Tohoku University

work.company: Graduate School of Information Sciences, Tohoku University
work.summary: Teaching Assistant for data science and machine learning courses. Supervisors: Prof. Kazunori Yamada, Assoc. Prof. Samy Baladram.
highlights: Data Science Training I (2024 spring, 2022 spring)
Data Science Skill Up Exercise (2022 spring)

education.area: Ph.D. Candidate, Information Sciences (Cognitive Neuroscience / Vision Science / Psychology)
courses: Thesis: Exploring Brain Mechanisms of Self-Initiated Attention Shift: Simultaneous Recording of EEG and Eye Movements
Advisors: Prof. Satoshi Shioiri, Prof. Shuichi Sakamoto, Assoc. Prof. Chia-huei Tseng

education.institution: Harvard Medical School / Massachusetts General Hospital
education.area: Visiting PhD Scholar — Department of Neurology, MGH
courses: Training in sEEG, hyperscanning, and neural mechanisms of speech and interactive communication
Advisors: Instructor Jing (Jill) Cai, Prof. Sydney S. Cash
Advisors: Prof. Satoshi Shioiri, Assoc. Prof. Chia-huei Tseng

publications.name: Task-constrained self-initiated attention shifts are indexed by frontal-midline theta ramping

teaching.course: Teaching Assistant — Data Science & Machine Learning Courses
teaching.institution: Graduate School of Information Sciences, Tohoku University
teaching.description: Supervised lab sessions, assisted students with programming assignments, and supported course delivery across 7 data science and machine learning courses (2022–2024). Supervisors: Prof. Kazunori Yamada, Assoc. Prof. Samy Baladram.
